items.final_wording: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.

source.title: Acute coronary syndromes
source.population: Adults with acute coronary syndromes, including people treated with percutaneous coronary intervention after myocardial infarction.
source.setting: Acute cardiology, discharge, cardiac rehabilitation, and post-myocardial-infarction follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for post-acute-coronary-syndrome and post-myocardial-infarction PCI care; partial for a generic post-PCI workflow because not every PCI follows acute coronary syndrome.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports review of investigation results and future management from discharge documentation, exercise and functional context, cardiac rehabilitation, and structured follow-up.

items.rationale: The NICE section supports documenting participation, individualized goals, progress, exercise and education context, patient questions, and follow-up in cardiac rehabilitation after acute coronary syndrome without generating an exercise or treatment plan.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-documented lifestyle discussion after myocardial infarction without autonomously generating diet, activity, smoking, or weight advice.

items.rationale: Lifestyle and secondary-prevention recommendations were reviewed for context, but no automatic advice or medicine content was mapped.
